rows.notes: Very clear printed year at top of page, unambiguous.

rows.description: Computus dni Rectoris J.P. Letchford a 2do die Novembris A.D. 1854 ad eundem diem revolution A.D. 1855
rows.notes: Long Latin/English heading; several words faint or abbreviated (e.g. 'dni', 'Recto ris'); transcription is best-effort following visible letter-forms (some letters uncertain).
Section title under ruled line; handwriting for 'Arrearagia' is slightly ambiguous (could be 'Arrearagia' or 'Arrearagia recepta'), chosen reading matches ink strokes and spacing.
Pounds and shillings columns clearly aligned to the vertical rules; only two numerals visible (19 and 5) so no pence written — interpreted as blank pence column.
All three numeric columns (6 | 18 | 9) are distinct and clear; shillings (18) and pence (9) are within valid ranges.
Three-column numeric group '12 15 5' clearly aligned; digits legible and valid.
Numbers '30 9 6' are well aligned in the three currency columns. The text '2 yr' after 'corn' is faint but apparent; small uncertainty in the exact abbreviation for '2 yr'.
Pounds (58) and shillings (5) visible; no pence digit present. 'Lady Day 1854' visible though slightly faded; allocation of the phrase is clear.
Large pounds figure '214' is clearly written and aligned with pounds column; shillings '13' and pence '4' appear in the correct columns and are legible.
Indented sub-item under Keddington Rectory; numeric group '19 2 6' clear. The word 'money' is short and slightly faded but context makes it certain this is the money sub-item.
Sub-item label 'Capons & Brawn' readable; amounts '2 12 9' clearly in their respective columns and valid.
Numbers '21 6 1' visible but the pence '1' is slightly faint and close to a stray ink mark; chosen reading fits alignment with column lines.
Three-part amount '4 8 3' is clear and correctly aligned with the rule lines; 'Cottages' word is legible.
Indented heading 'Keddington Mills' then 'Money' and 'Corn' visible; numeric '17 16 3' aligns well with columns and is legible.
Place name introducing a grouped set of sub-entries; the parenthetical 'late Norris' is legible though slightly faded.
Amount appears as '17 3 2 1/2' — pence digit '2' and a half-penny mark (looks like 'ob' / small loop). Fraction interpreted as 0.5; fraction symbol is somewhat faint but matches halfpenny form.
Numeric trio '32 19 5' is clear and well aligned; the descriptor '1/4 of remaining Rent' is readable though cramped.
Small place heading (spelled 'Little Iew' on page). The exact letter of the second word is slightly stylised but reading 'Iew' matches the visible strokes.
Three numbers appear '6 5 8'; the pounds digit '6' and shillings '5' are clear; pence '8' a little cramped but legible in pence column.
The entry 'timber' (abbreviated) is readable; numeric '3 19 6' clearly aligned and valid.

Single '9' appears in the pounds column for this line but the context is ambiguous (could be 9 pounds or 9 shillings depending on faint alignment). Chosen as 9 pounds because the digit aligns with the pounds column; however the ink is light and placement not perfectly clear.
Large pounds figure '108' is clear and aligns with pounds column; '9' and '10' for shillings/pence respectively are legible.
Sub-entry 'corn' under Long Wittenham; numeric '26 15 6' is clear and valid.

Only a solitary '3' visible on this line in the pounds column; shillings and pence not written. The 'Rec'd Land Tax' label is faint; chosen as £3 0s 0d given alignment but faintness lowers confidence.

Line reads 'Do. Cottages' (do = ditto to previous entry) but there is no clearly associated numeric group visible on the same horizontal line — ink/spacing make it unclear whether a numeric follows or it is blank here. Serious ambiguity so left amounts blank.
Place name appears as 'Medenham' or 'Medonham' — letters slightly stylised; chosen 'Medenham' as best visual match to the page.
Numeric '6 5 8' appears clearly to the right of 'Medenham money 1st'. Alignment consistent with pounds/shillings/pence columns. Minor uncertainty in the small script for 'Medenham'.

Amount appears as '16 10 1/2' — shillings '10' clear, pence shows '1' with a half-penny symbol (loop) after it. Fraction interpreted as 0.5. Slight fade on the half-penny mark but form matches other half-penny notations on the page.
Final aggregate at bottom right appears as '618 1 2' (a dot precedes the 618 but this is a typographic/formatting mark). The three numeric columns align with the rule lines and are legible.
